title: The Big Picture: Russia's challenges
summary:
Main
By Mary Melton
Lack of buyers, lack of tankers
Russian liftings of crude oil have fallen by 40% since the US sanctions on Rosneft and Lukoil were announced, even though the sanctions don't come into force until the end of this week. This is because buyers are either avoiding Russian oil or waiting for heavier price discounts. Despite the loss of exports, Russian demand for tankers is up by 6% today compared to the period immediately prior to the sanctions.
